title: 3D completion and overlay parity
description: Bring the required 3D capability to v0.1 acceptance: base view availability, lazy bundle/fallback behavior, drag rotate, pan, wheel zoom, reset, fit, visible gizmo, zone/layer/kind placement, and auth/dataflow/boundary/permission/validation overlay parity where practical.
triage_notes: Cut from product principles §6, 03 3D view, and 04 engine acceptance. Completion: core fallback remains, real 3D bundle passes interaction/framing tests, and overlay-relevant model elements are visible or gracefully degraded in 3D. Use test/fixtures/pattern-samples as acceptance input for 3D/isometric framing and overlay parity because the set covers small, medium, large hybrid, and Android architectures. 2026-06-27 Codex update: playground/viewer controls now expose overview/layer and 2d/3d only; the playground template includes auth/dataflow/boundary/permission/validation metadata; 3D consumes overlay projection for emphasized edges, permission overlay edges, node badges, boundary volumes, icons, and corner-offset zone labels. 2026-06-27 Codex update: 2D routing now treats component-border coincidence as a hard route cost and regression; ports are redistributed across opposite faces, endpoint lanes are deconflicted, fallback safety routes prefer distributed ports, and final endpoint/bend cleanup preserves no-node-hit/no-border-hit invariants. Verified on comprehensive + pattern samples: borderHits=0 across all checked fixtures. 2026-06-27 Codex update: added rendered SVG port validation plumbing (src/render-validation.ts) and wired it into pattern CLI/browser verifiers. Validation checks exact endpoint overlap per node, same-side minimum port gap, missing rendered node metadata, long same-line segment overlap, and component-region intersections per rendered base view + overlay combination; path segment parsing respects SVG M breaks. Routing now repairs component-intersecting edges with shortest valid orthogonal routes across candidate source/target ports, then decongests final endpoints. Permission overlay lines are summarized into target-node badges instead of adding dense synthetic routes. Verification: npm test (141 passing), npm run build, npm run verify:pattern-samples (6 samples x 3 base views x 32 overlay sets = 576 2D renders, failures=[]).
